items.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure

items.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
items.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it s expensive.Claude Code, Anthropic s terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But

items.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
items.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg s $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billb

items.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
items.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company s workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data,

items.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
items.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and

items.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
items.description: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four da

items.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds

items.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
items.description: MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.

items.title: Money transfer app Duc exposed thousands of driver’s licenses and passports to the open web
items.description: An exposed Amazon-hosted server allowed anyone to access reams of customer data without needing a password.

items.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
items.description: Google is adding a way to customize and instruct avatars for video creation in the Vids app.

items.title: ICE says it bought Paragon’s spyware to use in drug trafficking cases
items.description: The acting director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement told lawmakers that the use of Paragon spyware is necessary to counter terrorists’ “thriving exploitation of encrypted communications platforms.”

items.title: United’s mobile app now shows TSA wait times at select airports
items.description: The TSA wait times feature is one of many new additions to the United app, including automatic rebooking assistance and real-time time weather radar maps.

items.title: Tesla’s cheaper vehicles aren’t helping its declining sales
items.description: The company's deliveries in the first quarter were just 6% higher than last year, and Tesla now faces a third straight year of falling sales.

items.title: Commonwealth Fusion Systems leans on magnets for near-term revenue
items.description: Realta Fusion is buying magnets from Commonwealth Fusion Systems, providing a revenue stopgap.

items.title: Diverse teams start with diverse VCs
items.description: It is the path of least resistance for a growth-stage company to hire from the familiar Silicon Valley pipelines but if a founder wants a diverse team, that value has to be put into practice from the very first hire.

items.title: Beehiiv expands into podcasting, taking aim at Patreon
items.description: One way Beehiiv is convincing creators to switch from rivals like Substack and Patreon is by not taking a cut of revenue.

items.title: Cash App launches ‘pay later’ feature for P2P pay transfers
items.description: Block says that the new feature has strong built-in protections to keep users from so-called debt spirals.

items.title: De-fi platform Drift suspends deposits and withdrawals after millions in crypto stolen in hack
items.description: Blockchain trackers put the cryptocurrency heist in the hundreds of millions of dollars and is already on track to be the largest crypto theft in 2026 so far.

items.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
items.description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

items.title: The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
items.description: Delve faces new allegations that it violated the open source license of its customer, Sim.ai, by taking the customers's tool and passing it off as its own.

items.title: ‘System failure’ paralyzes Baidu robotaxis in China
items.description: Passengers in Baidu's robotaxis were trapped for up to two hours.

items.title: A new connections app, Sonder, has a deliberately tedious sign-up process (and it’s working)
items.description: Sonder profiles are completely unstructured, encouraging users to build something that looks like a mood board or a digital collage. Think MySpace rather than LinkedIn.
